question: q01
question_title: 信息流与新闻筛选模型
statement: Develop one or more model(s) that allow(s) you to explore the flow of information and filter or find what qualifies as news.
methods: 只使用官方题面给出的五个传播时期，构造 media access、transmission speed、network connectivity、gatekeeping filter 和 channel capacity 的显式时代参数；用加权 news score 判断 presidential assassination、war news、celebrity rumor、local storm、viral video 是否达到新闻阈值。对应模型：信息扩散、新闻价值评分、多指标综合评价、阈值分类。

data_source.source_pdf: /Users/wuxiaojun/code/Math-Modeling-World/docs/mcm-2015-2025/official_assets_extracted/2016/Measuring the Evolution and Influence in Society's Information Networks.pdf

period_descriptions.1870s_telegraph_train_newspaper: newspapers delivered by trains and stories passed by telegraph
period_descriptions.1920s_radio: radios became a more common household item
period_descriptions.1990s_early_internet: households began connecting to the early internet
period_descriptions.2010s_smartphone: we can carry a connection to the world on our phones

example_events_named_in_statement: Taylor Swift engagement rumor if it had happened in 1860

assumptions.era_coefficients: media_access, transmission_speed, network_connectivity, gatekeeping_filter, and channel_capacity are transparent normalized assumptions anchored to the five eras named in the official PDF.
assumptions.awareness_model: awareness(t) = reachable_population * (1 - exp(-diffusion_rate * t)); diffusion_rate increases with speed, connectivity, information value, and source credibility.
assumptions.news_filter_rule: news_score = 0.34 value + 0.24 source credibility + 0.18 affected population + 0.14 novelty + 0.10 shareability; items above threshold qualify as news.
assumptions.opinion_model: A one-step DeGroot-style influence score estimates opinion shift from value, source credibility, bias alignment, message format, and network strength.
assumptions.capacity_projection: 2050 capacity extrapolates log channel capacity against era year and caps the multiplier to avoid claiming an unbounded physical law.

periods.official_description: newspapers delivered by trains and stories passed by telegraph
radios became a more common household item
households began connecting to the early internet
we can carry a connection to the world on our phones

news_filter_model.definition: weighted information value and social transmissibility filter

assumption_audit.truthfulness_note: This workflow uses official statement parameters and explicit deterministic assumptions; it does not claim that the normalized era coefficients are observed data.

replaceable_assumptions.era_coefficients: media_access, transmission_speed, network_connectivity, gatekeeping_filter, and channel_capacity are transparent normalized assumptions anchored to the five eras named in the official PDF.

replaceable_assumptions.news_filter_rule: news_score = 0.34 value + 0.24 source credibility + 0.18 affected population + 0.14 novelty + 0.10 shareability; items above threshold qualify as news.
replaceable_assumptions.opinion_model: A one-step DeGroot-style influence score estimates opinion shift from value, source credibility, bias alignment, message format, and network strength.
replaceable_assumptions.capacity_projection: 2050 capacity extrapolates log channel capacity against era year and caps the multiplier to avoid claiming an unbounded physical law.
